overview: Rakdos Midrange in Modern leverages efficient removal and board control to outvalue opponents over multiple turns. By combining targeted disruption with creature-based pressure, the deck aims to stabilize early and close games with resilient threats. The provided signature cards exemplify this approach through board wipes, life drain, and flexible interaction.
win_conditions: Trading up with Orcish Bowmasters to clear opposing boards while preserving life total
Using Moonshadow to enable aggressive pushes with smaller creatures while restricting opponent blockers
Grinding out life with Burning Inquiry to force unfavorable trades and close games

keep_criteria: Hands with at least two lands and a removal spell or early creature
Draws containing Burning Inquiry or Orcish Bowmasters paired with mana sources
Combinations that allow for immediate board presence or disruption
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (prioritizing duals/shocks or basic swamps/stacks for fixing), at least one removal spell (e.g., Fatal Push, Go for the Throat), and a threat or card draw engine.
Keep hands that can play a turn-1 land and a turn-2 threat or interaction.
mulligan_criteria: Mana screws with three or more lands and no interaction
Hands lacking removal or early game answers
Draws with only high-cost cards and no ramp or draw
Mulligan on 3+ lands with no plays, hands with only removal and no threats/lands, or hands with 0-1 lands.

early_game: Play lands and deploy early creatures to establish board presence
Use Burning Inquiry to disrupt opponent's opening turns and gain life
Hold removal for key threats while developing your own board
If you have a cheap threat or discard spell and the opponent is aggressive, consider playing it to disrupt their curve.
Turn 2: Develop mana, cast your 2-drop threat, or use removal on an early aggressive threat.
Land sequencing prioritizes fixing to ensure you can cast your 2-drop and 3-drop on curve without flooding.
Always prioritize playing a land over casting a spell if you are one mana short of a key play.
mid_game: Utilize Orcish Bowmasters to clear opposing boards and generate value
Cast Moonshadow to restrict blocking and push damage with your creatures
Trade efficiently to maintain board advantage while drawing into more resources
Prioritize card advantage engines and efficient removal.
Don't overcommit mana on single-target spells if a board wipe or sweep is available.
Manage graveyard resources carefully if the format has graveyard hate, and avoid casting unnecessary spells that could be targeted by discard or removal.
Always maintain a hand of 3-4 cards to weather interaction.
Commit threats when you have removal to answer opponent's interaction or when they have no sweepers.
Hold back if you suspect a board wipe or heavy removal.
late_game: Stabilize with resilient threats and card advantage engines
Use life gain from Burning Inquiry to outlast attrition strategies
Close out games with efficient removal and board control
High-impact threats, card advantage engines that draw into game-ending spells, or direct damage spells.
Win by out-resourcing and applying pressure when the opponent has no answers.
Use discard to remove their last interaction, then commit your remaining threats to close the game.
When you have card advantage, removal for their interaction, and they have no sweepers or hard answers.
Go all-in when you can close the game in 1-2 turns, or when you have enough threats to overwhelm their remaining blockers.
Commit when you've successfully disrupted their combo or control game plan.

key_cards.name: Orcish Bowmasters
key_cards.notes: Cast on opponent's turn or when they have multiple creatures to maximize the death trigger. Use it to clear blockers before attacking or to remove key threats while preserving your life total.

key_cards.name: Moonshadow
key_cards.role: Board state manipulation and aggression enabler
key_cards.notes: Play when you have multiple small creatures to bypass blockers. Use it to force unfavorable trades and push damage through while restricting opponent's combat options.

key_cards.name: Burning Inquiry
key_cards.notes: Use to scout opponent's hand or library for key cards. Prioritize targeting high-value spells or threats. The life gain helps stabilize against aggressive decks and extends your game length.

sequencing_tips: Hold Burning Inquiry for opponent's key spells or when you need life to survive a push
Cast Orcish Bowmasters after opponent commits to a board state to maximize the death trigger
Play Moonshadow before attacking to force opponent to commit blockers or concede damage
Prioritize removal on threats that can't be blocked efficiently
Manage life total carefully to avoid being burned out by opponent's finishers
The listed signature cards (Orcish Bowmasters, Moonshadow, Burning Inquiry) do not match the B/R color identity of Rakdos Midrange and likely indicate a decklist error.
Verify your card list and ensure all cards are legal in your format.
Standard Rakdos Midrange relies on efficient removal, card draw, resilient threats, and discard.
Avoid overcommitting to the board without answers, and prioritize card advantage in longer games.
Practice mulligan decisions carefully, as Rakdos Midrange is highly sensitive to early mana and card quality.

matchups.advice: Prioritize life gain with Burning Inquiry and use Orcish Bowmasters to clear early boards. Play Moonshadow to restrict their aggressive pushes and buy time for your midrange threats.
Apply steady pressure with creatures and use removal to clear their counterspells or sweepers. Burn through their resources with efficient trades and close games before they stabilize.

Disrupt their key pieces with Burning Inquiry and hold removal for their combo enablers. Use Orcish Bowmasters to clear their board if they go wide, and apply pressure to force them to play around you.

Use cheap, efficient threats that are hard to counter, or cast spells when they have no mana up. Use discard to remove counterspells from hand. Avoid overcommitting to a single threat; have multiple lines of attack to pressure them into making mistakes.

Apply early pressure to disrupt their mana development. Use efficient removal to kill their ramp spells or early threats. Don't overextend into their late-game finishers; stabilize with removal and discard, then close the game before they can deploy their win conditions.

Win the resource war. Prioritize card advantage, efficient removal, and threat protection. Control the board with sweepers if available. Use discard to remove their key threats or interaction, and avoid overcommitting to the board without answers. The player who manages resources better and makes fewer mistakes will win.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers, counters, and graveyard recursion.
Note when opponents are holding up mana for interaction vs. developing their board.
Track key combo pieces or win conditions, and adjust your aggression accordingly.
Pay attention to when they tap out for a single spell, as this often indicates they are vulnerable to a threat or discard.
